  [tls]

I don't know

Gateway / Web UI

Pre-built binary

Ubuntu / Debian

I need to understand the infinite redirect loop between `/` and `/onboarding` when accessing moltis gateway via Tailscale reverse proxy. Tailscale Serve proxies HTTPS→HTTP.

Search thoroughly for:
1. The server routing code that handles `/` and `/onboarding` - look in `crates/web/src/server.rs` or similar
2. Any redirect logic that sends users to `/onboarding` or from `/onboarding` back to `/`
3. Any HTTPS/TLS detection logic that might cause redirects (checking scheme, X-Forwarded-Proto, etc.)
4. Any middleware that does redirects based on protocol or security
5. The onboarding completion check logic

Focus on finding the root cause of a redirect loop when behind a reverse proxy that terminates TLS.

    95→/// Memory snapshot included in gon data and tick broadcasts.
    96→#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
    97→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
    98→pub(crate) struct MemSnapshot {
    99→    process: u64,
   100→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   101→    local_llama_cpp: Option<u64>,
   102→    available: u64,
   103→    total: u64,
   104→}
   105→
   106→/// Collect a point-in-time memory snapshot (process RSS + local llama.cpp +
   107→/// system memory).
   108→pub(crate) fn collect_mem_snapshot() -> MemSnapshot {
   109→    let mut sys = sysinfo::System::new();
   110→    sys.refresh_memory();
   111→    let pid = sysinfo::get_current_pid().ok();
   112→    if let Some(pid) = pid {
   113→        sys.refresh_processes_specifics(
   114→            sysinfo::ProcessesToUpdate::Some(&[pid]),
   115→            false,
   116→            sysinfo::ProcessRefreshKind::nothing().with_memory(),
   117→        );
   118→    }
   119→    let process = pid
   120→        .and_then(|p| sys.process(p))
   121→        .map(|p| p.memory())
   122→        .unwrap_or(0);
   123→    let local_llama_cpp = moltis_gateway::server::local_llama_cpp_bytes_for_ui();
   124→    let total = sys.total_memory();
   125→    // available_memory() returns 0 on macOS; fall back to total − used.
   126→    let available = match sys.available_memory() {
   127→        0 => total.saturating_sub(sys.used_memory()),
   128→        v => v,
   129→    };
   130→    MemSnapshot {
   131→        process,
   132→        local_llama_cpp: (local_llama_cpp > 0).then_some(local_llama_cpp),
   133→        available,
   134→        total,
   135→    }
   136→}

   138→// ── Git branch ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139→
   140→fn detect_git_branch() -> Option<String> {
   141→    static BRANCH: std::sync::OnceLock<Option<String>> = std::sync::OnceLock::new();
   142→    BRANCH
   143→        .get_or_init(|| {
   144→            let repo = gix::discover(".").ok()?;
   145→            let head = repo.head().ok()?;
   146→            let branch = head.referent_name()?.shorten().to_string();
   147→            parse_git_branch(&branch)
   148→        })
   149→        .clone()
   150→}
   151→
   152→fn parse_git_branch(raw: &str) -> Option<String> {
   153→    let branch = raw.trim();
   154→    if branch.is_empty() || branch == "main" || branch == "master" {
   155→        None
   156→    } else {
   157→        Some(branch.to_owned())
   158→    }
   159→}

   161→const SESSION_PREVIEW_MAX_CHARS: usize = 200;
   162→
   163→fn truncate_preview(text: &str, max_chars: usize) -> String {
   164→    let mut chars = text.chars();
   165→    let mut out = String::new();
   166→    for _ in 0..max_chars {
   167→        if let Some(ch) = chars.next() {
   168→            out.push(ch);
   169→        } else {
   170→            return out;
   171→        }
   172→    }
   173→    if chars.next().is_some() {
   174→        out.push('…');
   175→    }
   176→    out
   177→}

   179→async fn build_recent_sessions_snapshot(gw: &GatewayState, limit: usize) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
   180→    let Some(ref metadata) = gw.services.session_metadata else {
   181→        return Vec::new();
   182→    };
   183→
   184→    let mut recent = Vec::new();
   185→    for entry in metadata.list().await.into_iter().take(limit) {
   186→        let active_channel = if let Some(ref binding_json) = entry.channel_binding {
   187→            if let Ok(target) =
   188→                serde_json::from_str::<moltis_channels::ChannelReplyTarget>(binding_json)
   189→            {
   190→                metadata
   191→                    .get_active_session(
   192→                        target.channel_type.as_str(),
   193→                        &target.account_id,
   194→                        &target.chat_id,
   195→                    )
   196→                    .await
   197→                    .map(|key| key == entry.key)
   198→                    .unwrap_or(false)
   199→            } else {
   200→                false
   201→            }
   202→        } else {
   203→            false
   204→        };
   205→        let preview = entry
   206→            .preview
   207→            .as_deref()
   208→            .map(|text| truncate_preview(text, SESSION_PREVIEW_MAX_CHARS));
   209→        let agent_id = entry.agent_id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "main".to_owned());
   210→        let agent_id_camel = agent_id.clone();
   211→
   212→        recent.push(serde_json::json!({
   213→            "id": entry.id,
   214→            "key": entry.key,
   215→            "label": entry.label,
   216→            "model": entry.model,
   217→            "createdAt": entry.created_at,
   218→            "updatedAt": entry.updated_at,
   219→            "messageCount": entry.message_count,
   220→            "lastSeenMessageCount": entry.last_seen_message_count,
   221→            "projectId": entry.project_id,
   222→            "sandbox_enabled": entry.sandbox_enabled,
   223→            "sandbox_image": entry.sandbox_image,
   224→            "worktree_branch": entry.worktree_branch,
   225→            "channelBinding": entry.channel_binding,
   226→            "activeChannel": active_channel,
   227→            "parentSessionKey": entry.parent_session_key,
   228→            "forkPoint": entry.fork_point,
   229→            "mcpDisabled": entry.mcp_disabled,
   230→            "preview": preview,
   231→            "archived": entry.archived,
   232→            "agent_id": agent_id,
   233→            "agentId": agent_id_camel,
   234→            "node_id": entry.node_id,
   235→            "version": entry.version,
   236→        }));
   237→    }
   238→
   239→    recent
   240→}

   244→#[derive(Debug, Default, serde::Serialize)]
   245→pub(crate) struct NavCounts {
   246→    projects: usize,
   247→    providers: usize,
   248→    channels: usize,
   249→    skills: usize,
   250→    mcp: usize,
   251→    crons: usize,

   254→
   255→pub(crate) async fn build_nav_counts(gw: &GatewayState) -> NavCounts {
   256→    let (projects, models, channels, mcp, crons) = tokio::join!(
   257→        gw.services.project.list(),
   258→        gw.services.model.list(),
   259→        gw.services.channel.status(),
   260→        gw.services.mcp.list(),
   261→        gw.services.cron.list(),
   262→    );
   263→
   264→    let projects = projects
   265→        .ok()
   266→        .and_then(|v| v.as_array().map(|a| a.len()))
   267→        .unwrap_or(0);
   268→
   269→    let providers = models
   270→        .ok()
   271→        .and_then(|v| {
   272→            v.as_array().map(|arr| {
   273→                let mut names: HashSet<&str> = HashSet::new();
   274→                for m in arr {
   275→                    if let Some(p) = m.get("provider").and_then(|p| p.as_str()) {
   276→                        names.insert(p);
   277→                    }
   278→                }
   279→                names.len()
   280→            })
   281→        })
   282→        .unwrap_or(0);
   283→
   284→    let channels = channels
   285→        .ok()
   286→        .and_then(|v| {
   287→            v.get("channels")
   288→                .and_then(|c| c.as_array())
   289→                .map(|a| a.len())
   290→        })
   291→        .unwrap_or(0);
   292→
   293→    let mut skills = 0usize;
   294→    if let Ok(path) = moltis_skills::manifest::ManifestStore::default_path() {
   295→        let store = moltis_skills::manifest::ManifestStore::new(path);
   296→        if let Ok(m) = store.load() {
   297→            skills = m
   298→                .repos
   299→                .iter()
   300→                .flat_map(|r| &r.skills)
   301→                .filter(|s| s.enabled)
   302→                .count();
   303→        }
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    let mcp = mcp
   307→        .ok()
   308→        .and_then(|v| {
   309→            v.as_array().map(|arr| {
   310→                arr.iter()
   311→                    .filter(|s| s.get("state").and_then(|s| s.as_str()) == Some("running"))
   312→                    .count()
   313→            })
   314→        })
   315→        .unwrap_or(0);
   316→
   317→    let crons = crons
   318→        .ok()
   319→        .and_then(|v| {
   320→            v.as_array().map(|arr| {
   321→                arr.iter()
   322→                    .filter(|j| {
   323→                        let enabled = j.get("enabled").and_then(|e| e.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
   324→                        let system = j.get("system").and_then(|s| s.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
   325→                        enabled && !system
   326→                    })
   327→                    .count()
   328→            })
   329→        })
   330→        .unwrap_or(0);
   331→
   332→    let hooks = gw.inner.read().await.discovered_hooks.len();
   333→
   334→    NavCounts {
   335→        projects,
   336→        providers,
   337→        channels,
   338→        skills,
   339→        mcp,
   340→        crons,
   341→        hooks,
   342→    }
   343→}

   347→pub(crate) async fn build_gon_data(gw: &GatewayState) -> GonData {
   348→    const GON_SESSIONS_RECENT_LIMIT: usize = 30;
   349→
   350→    let port = gw.port;
   351→    let identity = gw
   352→        .services
   353→        .onboarding
   354→        .identity_get()
   355→        .await
   356→        .ok()
   357→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   358→        .unwrap_or_default();
   359→
   360→    let counts = build_nav_counts(gw).await;
   361→    let (crons, cron_status) = tokio::join!(gw.services.cron.list(), gw.services.cron.status());
   362→    let crons: Vec<moltis_cron::types::CronJob> = crons
   363→        .ok()
   364→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   365→        .unwrap_or_default();
   366→    let cron_status: moltis_cron::types::CronStatus = cron_status
   367→        .ok()
   368→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   369→        .unwrap_or_default();
   370→    let (heartbeat_config, channels_offered) = {
   371→        let inner = gw.inner.read().await;
   372→        (
   373→            inner.heartbeat_config.clone(),
   374→            inner.channels_offered.clone(),
   375→        )
   376→    };
   377→    let channel_descriptors: Vec<moltis_channels::ChannelDescriptor> = channels_offered
   378→        .iter()
   379→        .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<moltis_channels::ChannelType>().ok())
   380→        .map(|ct| ct.descriptor())
   381→        .collect();
   382→
   383→    let heartbeat_runs: Vec<moltis_cron::types::CronRunRecord> = gw
   384→        .services
   385→        .cron
   386→        .runs(serde_json::json!({ "id": "__heartbeat__", "limit": 10 }))
   387→        .await
   388→        .ok()
   389→        .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
   390→        .unwrap_or_default();
   391→
   392→    let sandbox = if let Some(ref router) = gw.sandbox_router {
   393→        SandboxGonInfo {
   394→            backend: router.backend_name().to_owned(),
   395→            os: std::env::consts::OS,
   396→            default_image: router.default_image().await,
   397→            image_building: router
   398→                .building_flag
   399→                .load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed),
   400→        }
   401→    } else {
   402→        SandboxGonInfo {
   403→            backend: "none".to_owned(),
   404→            os: std::env::consts::OS,
   405→            default_image: moltis_tools::sandbox::DEFAULT_SANDBOX_IMAGE.to_owned(),
   406→            image_building: false,
   407→        }
   408→    };
   409→
   410→    // Fetch agent personas for the gon data.
   411→    let agents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = if let Some(ref store) = gw.services.agent_persona_store {
   412→        store
   413→            .list()
   414→            .await
   415→            .ok()
   416→            .map(|list| {
   417→                list.into_iter()
   418→                    .map(|a| serde_json::to_value(a).unwrap_or_default())
   419→                    .collect()
   420→            })
   421→            .unwrap_or_default()
   422→    } else {
   423→        Vec::new()
   424→    };
   425→
   426→    let sessions_recent = build_recent_sessions_snapshot(gw, GON_SESSIONS_RECENT_LIMIT).await;
   427→
   428→    GonData {
   429→        identity,
   430→        version: gw.version.clone(),
   431→        port,
   432→        counts,
   433→        crons,
   434→        cron_status,
   435→        heartbeat_config,
   436→        heartbeat_runs,
   437→        voice_enabled: cfg!(feature = "voice"),
   438→        graphql_enabled: cfg!(feature = "graphql"),
   439→        git_branch: detect_git_branch(),
   440→        mem: collect_mem_snapshot(),
   441→        deploy_platform: gw.deploy_platform.clone(),
   442→        channels_offered,
   443→        channel_descriptors,
   444→        update: gw.inner.read().await.update.clone(),
   445→        sandbox,
   446→        routes: SPA_ROUTES.clone(),
   447→        started_at: *PROCESS_STARTED_AT_MS,
   448→        openclaw_detected: moltis_gateway::server::openclaw_detected_for_ui(),
   449→        sessions_recent,
   450→        agents,
   451→        #[cfg(feature = "vault")]
   452→        vault_status: {
   453→            if let Some(ref vault) = gw.vault {
   454→                match vault.status().await {
   455→                    Ok(s) => format!("{s:?}").to_lowercase(),
   456→                    Err(_) => "error".to_owned(),
   457→                }
   458→            } else {
   459→                "disabled".to_owned()
   460→            }
   461→        },
   462→    }
   463→}

   540→pub(crate) fn script_safe_json<T: serde::Serialize>(value: &T) -> String {
   541→    let json = match serde_json::to_string(value) {
   542→        Ok(json) => json,
   543→        Err(e) => {
   544→            warn!(error = %e, "failed to serialize gon data for html template");
   545→            "{}".to_owned()
   546→        },
   547→    };
   548→    json.replace('<', "\\u003c")
   549→        .replace('>', "\\u003e")
   550→        .replace('&', "\\u0026")
   551→        .replace('\u{2028}', "\\u2028")
   552→        .replace('\u{2029}', "\\u2029")
   553→}

   594→pub(crate) async fn render_spa_template(
   595→    gateway: &GatewayState,
   596→    template: SpaTemplate,
   597→) -> axum::response::Response {
   598→    let (build_ts, asset_prefix) = if is_dev_assets() {
   599→        let ts = std::time::SystemTime::now()
   600→            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   601→            .unwrap_or_default()
   602→            .as_millis();
   603→        ("dev".to_owned(), format!("/assets/v/{ts}/"))
   604→    } else {
   605→        static HASH: std::sync::LazyLock<String> = std::sync::LazyLock::new(asset_content_hash);
   606→        (HASH.to_string(), format!("/assets/v/{}/", *HASH))
   607→    };
   608→
   609→    let nonce = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
   610→
   611→    // Resolve Shiki URL from config override or default CDN.
   612→    let shiki_url = gateway
   613→        .inner
   614→        .read()
   615→        .await
   616→        .shiki_cdn_url
   617→        .clone()
   618→        .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_SHIKI_CDN_URL.to_owned());
   619→
   620→    let body = match template {
   621→        SpaTemplate::Index => {
   622→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   623→            let share_meta = build_share_meta(&gon.identity);
   624→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   625→            let template = IndexHtmlTemplate {
   626→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   627→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   628→                nonce: &nonce,
   629→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   630→                share_title: &share_meta.title,
   631→                share_description: &share_meta.description,
   632→                share_site_name: &share_meta.site_name,
   633→                share_image_url: SHARE_IMAGE_URL,
   634→                share_image_alt: &share_meta.image_alt,
   635→                routes: &SPA_ROUTES,
   636→                shiki_url: &shiki_url,
   637→            };
   638→            match template.render() {
   639→                Ok(html) => html,
   640→                Err(e) => {

Now I have the complete picture! Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Summary

I've identified the root cause of the infinite redirect loop between `/` and `/onboarding` when accessing Moltis gateway via Tailscale reverse proxy. Here are the key findings:

### **Core Problem**

When Tailscale Serve proxies HTTPS→HTTP (terminating TLS), the gateway cannot properly determine whether onboarding is needed, causing a redirect loop between `/` and `/onboarding`.

### **Root Cause Chain**

1. **Redirect Logic (crates/web/src/spa.rs, lines 17-38)**
   - `spa_fallback()` checks if user is onboarded
   - If not onboarded AND not on `/onboarding` path → redirect to `/onboarding`
   - `onboarding_handler()` checks if already onboarded
   - If onboarded → redirect to `/`
   - This creates the loop when the onboarding state detection is inconsistent

2. **Onboarding Detection (crates/web/src/templates.rs, lines 734-742)**
   ```rust
   pub(crate) async fn onboarding_completed(gw: &GatewayState) -> bool {
       gw.services
           .onboarding
           .wizard_status()
           .await
           .ok()
           .and_then(|v| v.get("onboarded").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()))
           .unwrap_or(false)
   }
   ```
   - Calls `wizard_status()` from `crates/onboarding/src/service.rs` (lines 141-155)
   - Returns true if `.onboarded` sentinel file exists OR `SKIP_ONBOARDING` env var is set

3. **Auth Status vs Onboarding Status Split**
   - **Setup Required** (auth_middleware.rs, line 116): Determined by checking if auth credentials exist
     - Returns `AuthResult::SetupRequired` when no credentials configured
   - **Onboarded** (onboarding_handler): Determined by checking sentinel file
   - These are **independent checks** that can become inconsistent
   
   The comment in auth_middleware.rs (lines 140-145) explains the fix from PR #310:
   ```rust
   // Allow all page paths through during setup — the SPA
   // handles onboarding redirects itself (spa_fallback
   // redirects to /onboarding when not yet onboarded).
   // This prevents a redirect loop when the instance is
   // already onboarded but auth credentials haven't been
   // configured yet (#310).
   ```

### **Why Tailscale Reverse Proxy Triggers This**

The issue manifests with Tailscale Serve because:

1. **HTTPS Termination**: Tailscale terminates TLS and proxies to HTTP backend
2. **No X-Forwarded-Proto Detection**: If the proxy doesn't add `X-Forwarded-Proto: https` header, the gateway can't distinguish between:
   - Direct HTTP requests (local development)
   - Proxied HTTPS requests (Tailscale)

3. **Client-Side Auth Check (crates/web/src/assets/js/app.js, lines 324-349)**
   ```javascript
   fetch("/api/auth/status")
       .then((r) => (r.ok ? r.json() : null))
       .then((auth) => {
           if (auth.setup_required) {
               window.location.assign("/onboarding");  // Line 333
               return;
           }
           // ...
       })
   ```
   - When setup is required BUT onboarding is complete, this redirects to `/onboarding`
   - Server then sees onboarded=true and redirects back to `/`
   - **Loop confirmed**

### **Key Code Locations**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `crates/web/src/spa.rs` | 17-38 | Server-side redirect handlers for SPA |
| `crates/web/src/templates.rs` | 722-742 | `should_redirect_to_onboarding()` and `onboarding_completed()` |
| `crates/onboarding/src/service.rs` | 44-58, 141-155 | Onboarding state detection via sentinel file |
| `crates/gateway/src/auth_middleware.rs` | 96-180 | Auth gate that blocks/allows routes |
| `crates/web/src/assets/js/app.js` | 302-349 | Client-side auth status polling + redirect |
| `crates/auth/src/locality.rs` | 40-66 | Local connection detection (checks for proxy headers, Host header, loopback IPs) |

### **Critical Decision Point**

The `behind_proxy` flag (`MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY` env var) is checked in `crates/auth/src/locality.rs:46` and immediately returns `false` for local connection detection when set. This prevents Tailscale Serve (which adds proxy headers) from bypassing auth when it shouldn't, BUT it also means:

- If `MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY=true` is set, the connection is never treated as "local"
- This is correct for security, but requires proper proxy header setup (X-Forwarded-For, Forwarded, etc.)

### **Why It's a Problem**

The two independent state sources create a race condition:
- **Onboarding state**: Determined by file existence (persistent, set once)
- **Auth state**: Determined by credential store (changeable, may not be set initially)

When these diverge (onboarded=true, auth_setup_required=true), the client-side redirect from auth status check conflicts with server-side redirect from onboarding check.

content.text: Now I have the full picture. Let me check the auth status endpoint to confirm.

Now the picture is clear. Here's the root cause analysis:

## Root Cause

The redirect loop happens because of a **state mismatch between onboarding and auth setup** when accessed via Tailscale (a reverse proxy).

Here's the chain:

1. User completes onboarding locally → sentinel file `.onboarded` is created
2. User accesses remotely via Tailscale Serve (HTTPS→HTTP proxy to `127.0.0.1:44937`)
3. Tailscale Serve proxies the request. The TCP source IP is `127.0.0.1` (loopback), **but** the `Host` header is the Tailscale domain (e.g., `machine.tail1234.ts.net`), not localhost

**What happens on each request cycle:**

1. **GET `/`** — `spa_fallback()` checks `onboarding_completed()` → true (sentinel file exists) → serves the SPA index page
2. **SPA loads**, JS fetches `GET /api/auth/status` → `auth_gate` runs `check_auth()`:
   - `is_local_connection()` returns `false` because `Host` header is non-loopback (`machine.tail1234.ts.net`)  
   - `store.is_setup_complete()` returns `false` if auth credentials (password) weren't configured during onboarding
   - → `AuthResult::SetupRequired`
   - Since path is `/api/auth/status` (starts with `/api/`), returns `401 { "code": "auth_setup_required" }`
3. **JS receives** `setup_required: true` → `window.location.assign("/onboarding")` (line 332 in app.js)
4. **GET `/onboarding`** → `onboarding_handler()` checks `onboarding_completed()` → true → `Redirect::to("/")`
5. **Back to step 1** → infinite loop

The core problem: Tailscale Serve on the same host creates a "bare proxy" scenario — TCP source is loopback but Host header is non-loopback. The `is_local_connection()` check correctly identifies this as non-local (line 56-61 of locality.rs), which then triggers `SetupRequired` for non-local connections when auth isn't configured yet.

## Suggested Fix

The fix should happen in `app.js` — when `setup_required` is true but we're already on a page that was served successfully (meaning onboarding IS complete), the client should redirect to `/login` instead of `/onboarding`, since the real issue is that auth credentials need to be set up, not that onboarding isn't done.

Alternatively (and probably better), the **server-side `auth_gate`** for the `SetupRequired` case on non-API page paths (line 139-150 of auth_middleware.rs) already lets the request through. The problem is the **client-side JS** then hits `/api/auth/status` which returns `setup_required` and redirects to `/onboarding`. The fix in `app.js`:

```javascript
// Line 332 in app.js - change:
if (auth.setup_required) {
    window.location.assign("/onboarding");
    return;
}

// To:
if (auth.setup_required) {
    if (window.location.pathname === "/onboarding") return; // already there
    window.location.assign("/onboarding");
    return;
}
```

But this alone won't break the loop because `/onboarding` server-side redirects to `/` when onboarded.

The **real fix** should be in `onboarding_handler` in `spa.rs` — when onboarding is complete but auth setup is not, the user needs to be sent to a page where they can set up auth credentials. Options:

1. **Best fix**: In `onboarding_handler`, when onboarded=true, serve the onboarding page anyway if auth setup is incomplete (so the user can set a password for remote access), instead of redirecting to `/`.

2. **Alternative**: In `app.js`, when `setup_required` is true, redirect to `/login` (where the setup UI could live) instead of `/onboarding`.

3. **Simplest pragmatic fix**: In `app.js`, when `setup_required` is true AND the page loaded successfully, show a message/modal telling the user to set up auth from the local machine, rather than redirecting.

Would you like me to implement one of these approaches? Option 1 seems most correct — the onboarding page should handle the "auth not yet configured" case even after the wizard is marked complete.

1. The onboarding JS code - likely in crates/web/src/assets/js/ - find the file that handles the onboarding wizard steps/pages
2. The onboarding HTML template (likely in crates/web/src/assets/ or templates)
3. The onboarding API endpoints (wizard_status, complete, etc.) in crates/onboarding/
4. How the wizard steps are defined and navigated (what are the steps? provider setup, auth setup, etc.)
5. The auth/password setup step specifically - how does it work in the onboarding flow?

I need to understand how to make the onboarding page show ONLY the auth setup step when onboarding is already complete but auth credentials aren't configured yet.

    46→/// Build the auth router with all `/api/auth/*` routes.
    47→pub fn auth_router() -> axum::Router<AuthState> {
    48→    axum::Router::new()
    49→        .route("/status", get(status_handler))
    50→        .route("/setup", post(setup_handler))
    51→        .route("/login", post(login_handler))
    52→        .route("/logout", post(logout_handler))
    53→        .route("/password/change", post(change_password_handler))
    54→        .route(
    55→            "/api-keys",
    56→            get(list_api_keys_handler).post(create_api_key_handler),
    57→        )
    58→        .route("/api-keys/{id}", delete(revoke_api_key_handler))
    59→        .route("/passkeys", get(list_passkeys_handler))
    60→        .route(
    61→            "/passkeys/{id}",
    62→            delete(remove_passkey_handler).patch(rename_passkey_handler),
    63→        )
    64→        .route(
    65→            "/passkey/register/begin",
    66→            post(passkey_register_begin_handler),
    67→        )
    68→        .route(
    69→            "/passkey/register/finish",
    70→            post(passkey_register_finish_handler),
    71→        )
    72→        .route("/passkey/auth/begin", post(passkey_auth_begin_handler))
    73→        .route("/passkey/auth/finish", post(passkey_auth_finish_handler))
    74→        .route(
    75→            "/setup/passkey/register/begin",
    76→            post(setup_passkey_register_begin_handler),
    77→        )
    78→        .route(
    79→            "/setup/passkey/register/finish",
    80→            post(setup_passkey_register_finish_handler),
    81→        )
    82→        .route("/reset", post(reset_auth_handler))
    83→        // Vault endpoints (encryption-at-rest).
    84→        .merge(vault_routes())
    85→}
    86→
    87→/// Build vault-specific routes (no-op when vault feature is disabled).
    88→#[cfg(feature = "vault")]
    89→fn vault_routes() -> axum::Router<AuthState> {
    90→    axum::Router::new()
    91→        .route("/vault/status", get(vault_status_handler))
    92→        .route("/vault/unlock", post(vault_unlock_handler))
    93→        .route("/vault/recovery", post(vault_recovery_handler))
    94→}
    95→
    96→#[cfg(not(feature = "vault"))]
    97→fn vault_routes() -> axum::Router<AuthState> {
    98→    axum::Router::new()
    99→}

### 2. **Web UI Onboarding Flow**

Location: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/tailscale-redirects/crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js` (lines 3250-3305)

The web UI dynamically builds steps based on configuration:

**Full step sequence (when available):**
1. **Security (Auth)** - Password/Passkey setup
2. **Import** (optional) - OpenClaw import if detected
3. **LLM** - Provider setup
4. **Voice** (optional) - Speech-to-text/text-to-speech
5. **Channel** - Telegram/Teams integration
6. **Identity** - User name & agent name/emoji/theme
7. **Summary** - Configuration review

**Dynamic step calculation** (lines 3251-3268):
```javascript
var openclawDetected = getGon("openclaw_detected") === true;
var allLabels = [t("onboarding:steps.security")];
if (openclawDetected) allLabels.push(t("onboarding:steps.import"));
allLabels.push(t("onboarding:steps.llm"));
if (voiceAvailable) allLabels.push(t("onboarding:steps.voice"));
allLabels.push(t("onboarding:steps.channel"), t("onboarding:steps.identity"), t("onboarding:steps.summary"));
var steps = authNeeded ? allLabels : allLabels.slice(1);  // Remove Security if not needed
```

### 3. **Auth Step Determination Logic**

Location: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/tailscale-redirects/crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js` (lines 3222-3242)

```javascript
useEffect(() => {
    fetch("/api/auth/status")
        .then((r) => (r.ok ? r.json() : null))
        .then((auth) => {
            if (auth?.setup_required || (auth?.auth_disabled && !auth?.localhost_only)) {
                setAuthNeeded(true);
                setAuthSkippable(!auth.setup_required);
                setStep(0);  // Start at Auth step
            } else {
                setAuthNeeded(false);
                ensureWsConnected();
                setStep(1);  // Skip Auth, start at LLM or Import
            }
        })
        // ...
}, []);
```

**Auth is shown (step 0) when:**
- `setup_required` is true (auth credentials not yet configured)
- OR `auth_disabled` is false AND not `localhost_only`

**Auth is skipped when:**
- `setup_required` is false (credentials already configured)
- AND either auth is disabled OR it's localhost-only

### 4. **Auth Step Components**

Location: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/tailscale-redirects/crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js` (lines 135-562)

content.text:
All compiles. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Fix Summary

**3 files changed** to break the infinite redirect loop when accessing moltis via Tailscale Serve:

### Root cause
When accessing remotely via Tailscale, the connection is non-local, so `check_auth()` returns `SetupRequired` (auth credentials not yet configured). The JS client redirects to `/onboarding`, but the server sees onboarding is already complete and redirects back to `/` → infinite loop.

### Changes

1. **`crates/web/src/spa.rs`** — `onboarding_handler` no longer redirects away from `/onboarding` when auth setup is still pending. This breaks the server-side half of the redirect loop.

2. **`crates/web/src/templates.rs`** — Added `auth_setup_pending` boolean to `GonData`, computed from the credential store. Injected into the page so the JS knows whether this is a "full onboarding" or "auth-only" scenario.

3. **`crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js`** — When `auth_setup_pending` is true (from gon) and `setup_required` is true (from `/api/auth/status`), the wizard shows **only the auth/security step** (no step indicator, no other wizard steps). After auth setup completes, it navigates directly to chat.

### Flow after fix
1. Remote user hits `/` → JS fetches auth status → `setup_required` → redirects to `/onboarding`
2. Server serves the onboarding page (doesn't redirect back because auth is pending)
3. JS detects auth-only mode → shows only the password/passkey setup step
4. User sets up auth → navigates to chat

content.content:
# Entire: A Developer Platform for AI Agent Context

## What is Entire?

Entire is a developer platform that integrates with your Git workflow to preserve AI agent sessions. According to the documentation, it "hooks into your Git workflow to capture AI agent sessions on every push, unifying your code with its context and reasoning."

## Core Functionality

The platform introduces **Checkpoints**, described as "a new primitive that automatically captures agent context as first-class, versioned data in Git." When developers commit code generated by an agent, Entire captures the complete session metadata alongside the commit.

## What Gets Captured and Stored

When you commit agent-generated code, Entire records:
- Session transcripts
- Original prompts and requests
- Files that were modified
- Token usage metrics
- Tool calls made during the session

This captured data becomes "searchable, shareable, and permanent."

## Git Integration and Storage

The platform operates as a "Git-native" solution where "every agent conversation is captured and indexed on Git push." The documentation emphasizes this is "local-first" technology that "runs entirely within your environment" with "no hosted services required."

## Supported Integrations

Entire currently supports Claude Code and Gemini, with additional integrations listed as coming soon, including OpenCode, Factory Droid, and others.

The entire CLI itself is open source under an MIT license and available on GitHub.

# Entire Directory Structure and Local Files

## Directory Creation

When you initialize Entire, it creates a `.entire` directory with this structure:

```
.entire/
├── settings.json        # Project settings (committed)
├── settings.local.json  # Local overrides (gitignored)
└── .gitignore          # Ensures local settings aren't committed
```

## File Purposes

**Project Settings** (`.entire/settings.json`): This is committed to version control and contains shared configuration like enabled status, log level, telemetry preference, and strategy options that apply to all team members.

**Local Settings** (`.entire/settings.local.json`): As stated in the documentation, this file is "automatically added to `.gitignore`" and stores personal preferences. The system merges local settings with project settings, allowing individual developers to override shared configurations without affecting the repository.

## Gitignore Handling

The `.entire/.gitignore` file is automatically created to ensure that "local settings aren't committed" to the repository. This prevents personal configuration choices from being tracked in version control.

## Key Point on Data Storage

The documentation emphasizes that Entire "looks for configuration in the following locations (in order of precedence)": local settings override project settings, which override defaults. No data storage beyond these configuration files is mentioned in the provided content.

Now I have the answer. According to the Entire docs:

**The `.entire/` directory should be partially committed.** The structure is:

```
.entire/
├── settings.json        # ← Committed (shared project config)
├── settings.local.json  # ← Gitignored automatically
├── .gitignore           # ← Ensures local files aren't committed
├── metadata/            # ← Gitignored (in your .entire/.gitignore)
└── tmp/                 # ← Gitignored (in your .entire/.gitignore)
```

The `.entire/.gitignore` already handles ignoring the local-only parts (`tmp/`, `metadata/`, `settings.local.json`). The intent is that `settings.json` (shared project settings) **is committed** so team members share the same Entire configuration.

content.content:
# Entire Configuration Settings

## Available Configuration Options

Based on the documentation, here are all configurable settings across Entire's three-tier system:

### Project Settings (`.entire/settings.json`)

| Setting | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|---------|
| `enabled` | boolean | `true` | Activates or deactivates Entire in the repository |
| `log_level` | string | `"info"` | Controls logging verbosity with values: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |
| `telemetry` | boolean | `null` | Analytics opt-in: `null` (prompt later), `true` (enabled), `false` (disabled) |
| `strategy_options` | object | `{}` | Additional checkpoint configuration options |

### Local Settings (`.entire/settings.local.json`)

Local settings use the same available options as project settings but are gitignored. As noted, "Local settings are merged with project settings. You only need to specify the settings you want to override."

### Global Settings (`~/.config/entire/settings.json`)

| Setting | Type | Default |
|---------|------|---------|
| `telemetry` | boolean | `false` |

## Environment Variable Overrides

- `ENTIRE_ENABLED` — disables Entire globally when set to `false`
- `ENTIRE_LOG_LEVEL` — overrides log verbosity
- `ENTIRE_TELEMETRY` — controls telemetry state

## Configuration Precedence

Priority order (highest to lowest): Local settings → Project settings → Global settings → Defaults

input.prompt: Extract ALL content. What are checkpoints? What strategy options are available? What can be configured about how checkpoints work?

content.content:
# Checkpoints Overview

## What Are Checkpoints?

Checkpoints are permanent captures of AI coding sessions created automatically when you make Git commits. According to the documentation, "Entire captures AI coding sessions as you work, and creates permanent checkpoints when you make Git commits."

The system attaches session data to commits and adds a checkpoint ID as a trailer (e.g., `Entire-Checkpoint: a3b2c4d5e6f7`). Session logs and metadata are stored in a dedicated `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch.

## Available Strategy Options

The documentation identifies one configurable strategy option:

**Summarize** - When enabled, the system uses AI to automatically generate session summaries during commits. The configuration appears as:

```json
{
  "strategy_options": {
    "summarize": {
      "enabled": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Configurable Features

The documentation mentions these configuration aspects:

1. **Auto-Summarize** - Can be enabled/disabled to generate AI-powered session summaries
2. **Summary Contents** - When active, summaries include accomplishments, key decisions, and modified files
3. **Authentication** - Auto-summarize requires Claude CLI installation and authentication
4. **Performance** - Summary generation occurs asynchronously without blocking commits

The documentation does not specify additional strategy options beyond the summarize feature.

message.content: Look at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/356 comments from greptile. Also thinking about it I think there are issues with your change, if someone has no auth and enables tailscale and bind, then the first user connecting could set auth. I think it would be best to have a "Access denied" when onboarding was already set, and let the user set auth either through terminal cli, or connecting the the interface via localhost.

content.content:
---
crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js:3235
**`authOnly` activates for fresh remote installs, skipping full wizard**

`getGon("auth_setup_pending")` is `true` whenever the credential store says auth isn't configured — it does **not** check whether onboarding has actually been completed. This means a brand-new user who accesses the server for the first time via Tailscale Serve will also land in `authOnly` mode: they'll see only the auth step, set a password/passkey, get sent to `preferredChatPath()`, and arrive at the chat page with no LLM provider or identity configured.

The backend `onboarding_handler` correctly guards against this with `should_redirect_from_onboarding(onboarded) && !auth_setup_pending`, but the client-side check has no awareness of `onboarded`.

Fix: either expose `onboarded` status in gon data and gate the flag in JS, **or** make `auth_setup_pending` in `build_gon_data` incorporate the onboarding completion check (mirroring the backend guard):

```rust
// in build_gon_data
let onboarded = onboarding_completed(gw).await;
// ...
auth_setup_pending: onboarded
    && gw
        .credential_store
        .as_ref()
        .is_some_and(|store| !store.is_setup_complete() && !store.is_auth_disabled()),
```

With that change the gon flag would be `false` for a fresh install, `authOnly` would never be set, and the full wizard would show as expected.
---
crates/web/src/spa.rs:40
**Duplicated `auth_setup_pending` logic**

The exact same credential-store predicate is computed independently in `onboarding_handler` here and again inside `build_gon_data` in `templates.rs` (line 452–455). If the condition ever needs to change (e.g. to incorporate the `onboarded` check suggested above), both sites must be updated in sync, which is easy to miss.

Consider extracting it into a shared helper — for example a free function in `templates.rs` or a method on `GatewayState` — and calling it from both places.

content.text:
Good catches from Greptile. And you're absolutely right about the security concern — letting the first remote user set auth is a real problem. Let me revert the approach entirely.

The safer design: when onboarding is done but auth isn't configured, remote users should see an "access denied" page telling them to set up auth locally (via localhost or CLI). No auth setup form exposed remotely.

Now let me look at the auth middleware to understand where to intercept. The fix should be: when `SetupRequired` and the request is non-local, return a proper "access denied" page instead of the 401 that triggers the redirect loop.
Now let me check what templates exist — I'll need a "setup required" page similar to the login page.

The redirect approach I just implemented actually does this cleanly: the middleware redirects to `/setup-required` (a public path), and the web crate renders the template. The redirect to `/setup-required` is the simpler equivalent — same separation of concerns, the middleware doesn't know about HTML, and the web crate handles rendering.
